```{r plot top drug across technologies}
library(gt)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(here)
library(purrr)

predicted_drugs = read.delim(here("data/v2/tableS2/final_high_confidence_drug_predictions.tsv"),sep="\t")
predicted_drugs
```

```{r get fraction of signatures each drug reverses}
# read in result files from different scores
# microarray (6 signatures) and RNAseq (29 signatures) separately
# but we have many connectivity scores; take median?
# report signature fractions for all scores

library(tidyverse)

all_scores_marray_df <- read_tsv(here("data/v2/tableS2/microarray_drug_score_occurrence.tsv"))
all_scores_rnaseq_df <- read_tsv(here("data/v2/tableS2/rnaseq_drug_score_occurrence.tsv"))

```

```{r clean up}
# add prefixes to distinguish columns
all_scores_marray_df <- all_scores_marray_df %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("microarray_", .x), .cols = -significant_drug)

all_scores_rnaseq_df <- all_scores_rnaseq_df %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("RNAseq_", .x), .cols = -significant_drug)
```

```{r combined signature fractions & scores | 2/3 for both technologies}
library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(stringr)

# join first (without replace_na)
# Combine microarray and RNA-seq results by union of drugs
combined_scores_df <- full_join(all_scores_marray_df, all_scores_rnaseq_df, by = "significant_drug")

# now safely apply replace_na() on all columns except unique_pert
replace_list <- as.list(setNames(rep(0, ncol(combined_scores_df) - 1), names(combined_scores_df)[-1]))
combined_scores_df <- replace_na(combined_scores_df, replace_list)

# unique_pert matches drug_name
combined_with_rank_df <- combined_scores_df %>%
  left_join(predicted_drugs, by = c("significant_drug" = "drug_name"))

# Step 1: Manually specify the exact columns to keep
score_cols <- c(
  "microarray_CMAP", "microarray_NCS", "microarray_Tau", "microarray_WCS", "microarray_Cor_pearson", "microarray_Cor_spearman"
  ,
  "RNAseq_CMAP", "RNAseq_NCS", "RNAseq_Tau", "RNAseq_WCS", "RNAseq_Cor_pearson", "RNAseq_Cor_spearman"
  
)

# Step 1: Prepare the data
# The select() call now only includes significant_drug as the identifier.
filtered_df <- combined_with_rank_df %>%
  select(significant_drug, combined_z_score, all_of(score_cols)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(combined_z_score)) %>%
  arrange(desc(combined_z_score)) %>%
  mutate(rank = row_number()) %>%
  relocate(rank, .before = everything())

# Step 2: Rename columns to use shorthand *before* creating the gt table.
final_table_df <- filtered_df %>%
  rename_with(~ str_replace(.x, "microarray_", "MA_")) %>%
  rename_with(~ str_replace(.x, "RNAseq_", "RS_"))

# Names for clean visualization
score_cols_short <- names(final_table_df)[grepl("^(MA_|RS_)", names(final_table_df))]
label_list <- purrr::set_names(str_remove_all(score_cols_short, "MA_|RS_"), score_cols_short)

# update names for correlations
label_list$MA_Cor_pearson <- "XCor"
label_list$MA_Cor_spearman <- "XSpe"
label_list$RS_Cor_pearson <- "XCor"
label_list$RS_Cor_spearman <- "XSpe"

# Step 3: Build the gt table with explicit widths and alignment
final_gt_table <- final_table_df %>%
  select(rank, combined_z_score, significant_drug, everything()) %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "rank") %>%
  tab_stubhead(label = "Rank") %>%
  
  cols_label(
    .list = label_list
  ) %>%

  # --- Spanners with UNIQUE IDs --- #
  tab_spanner(label = "CMAP1.0", columns = c(MA_CMAP), id = "ma_cmap1.0") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "CMAP2.0", columns = c(MA_NCS, MA_Tau, MA_WCS), id = "ma_cmap2.0") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Correlation", columns = c(MA_Cor_pearson, MA_Cor_spearman), id = "ma_corr") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Enrichment-based", columns = c(MA_CMAP, MA_NCS, MA_Tau, MA_WCS), id = "ma_enrich") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Correlation-based", columns = c(MA_Cor_pearson, MA_Cor_spearman), id = "ma_correlation") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Microarray (6 signatures)", columns = starts_with("MA_"), id = "ma_main") %>%
  
  tab_spanner(label = "CMAP1.0", columns = c(RS_CMAP), id = "rs_cmap1.0") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "CMAP2.0", columns = c(RS_NCS, RS_Tau, RS_WCS), id = "rs_cmap2.0") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Correlation", columns = c(RS_Cor_pearson, RS_Cor_spearman), id = "rs_corr") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Enrichment-based", columns = c(RS_CMAP, RS_NCS, RS_Tau, RS_WCS), id = "rs_enrich") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Correlation-based", columns = c(RS_Cor_pearson, RS_Cor_spearman), id = "rs_correlation") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "RNAseq (29 signatures)", columns = starts_with("RS_"), id = "rs_main") %>%
  
  data_color(
    columns = starts_with("MA_"),
    colors = scales::col_numeric(
      palette = c("mistyrose", "firebrick"),
      domain = NULL
    )
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = starts_with("RS_"),
    colors = scales::col_numeric(
      palette = c("lightblue", "blue"),
      domain = NULL
    )
  ) %>%
  
  fmt_number(
    columns = combined_z_score,
    decimals = 2
  ) %>%
  
  # 1. Set uniform widths
  cols_width(
    stub() ~ px(50), 
    significant_drug ~ px(180),
    combined_z_score ~ px(100),
    starts_with(c("MA_", "RS_")) ~ px(150) 
  ) %>%
  
  # 2. Center all data cells
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(combined_z_score, starts_with("MA_"), starts_with("RS_"))
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Center column labels using the backward-compatible tab_style()
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(align = "center"),
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = everything())
  )

# Now, save directly without modifying the table options
gtsave(final_gt_table, here("drugs_methodwise_final_table_count.html"))

# resize to fit into png
final_gt_table <- final_gt_table %>%
  tab_options(
    # Set a smaller font size for the entire table
    table.font.size = px(16),
    # Reduce the vertical padding in data rows to make them shorter
    data_row.padding = px(10)
  )

gtsave(
  final_gt_table,
  "final_drugs_methodwise_final_table_count.png",
  vwidth = 2500, # Adjust as needed
  expand = 10
) # Must manually convert to pdf

final_gt_table
```

```{r union --> intersect indiv and aggr}
# unique_pert matches drug_name
combined_scores_df <- full_join(all_scores_marray_df, all_scores_rnaseq_df, by = "significant_drug")
replace_list <- as.list(setNames(rep(0, ncol(combined_scores_df) - 1), names(combined_scores_df)[-1]))
combined_scores_df <- replace_na(combined_scores_df, replace_list)
combined_with_rank_df <- combined_scores_df %>%
  left_join(predicted_drugs, by = c("significant_drug" = "drug_name"))

combined_with_rank_df  <- combined_with_rank_df  %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~replace_na(.x, 0)))

# read in 140 drugs
drugs140 = read_delim("~/Documents/GitHub/drugrep_tb/results/uniformly_processed/intersect_RAresult_drugs.tsv",delim = '\t')
combined_with_rank_df = combined_with_rank_df[combined_with_rank_df$unique_pert %in% drugs140$intersect_RAresult_drugs,]

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)

# Step 1: Manually specify the exact columns to keep
score_cols <- c(
  "microarray_CMAP", "microarray_NCS", "microarray_Tau", "microarray_WCS", "microarray_Cor_pearson", "microarray_Cor_spearman"
  ,
  "RNAseq_CMAP", "RNAseq_NCS", "RNAseq_Tau", "RNAseq_WCS", "RNAseq_Cor_pearson", "RNAseq_Cor_spearman"
  
)

# Step 2: Select only those columns + MeanRank and unique_pert
filtered_df <- combined_with_rank_df %>%
  select(MeanRank, unique_pert, all_of(score_cols)) %>%
  arrange(desc(MeanRank))  # Show higher ranks first


filtered_df <- filtered_df %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "unique_pert") %>%
  
  ### Microarray nested spanners
  # CMAP1.0 (classic CMAP score)
  tab_spanner(
    label = "CMAP1.0",
    columns = c(microarray_CMAP),
    id = "marray_cmap1"
  ) %>%
  
  # CMAP2.0 (connectivity scores)
  tab_spanner(
    label = "LINCS",
    columns = c(microarray_NCS, microarray_Tau, microarray_WCS),
    id = "marray_cmap2"
  ) %>%
  
  # Correlation scores
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Correlation",
    columns = c(microarray_Cor_pearson, microarray_Cor_spearman),
    id = "marray_corr"
  ) %>%
  
  # Method class (enrichment-based includes CMAP1.0 + CMAP2.0)
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Enrichment-based",
    columns = c(microarray_CMAP, microarray_NCS, microarray_Tau, microarray_WCS),
    id = "marray_enrich"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Correlation-based",
    columns = c(microarray_Cor_pearson, microarray_Cor_spearman),
    id = "marray_correlation"
  ) %>%
  
  # Platform
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Microarray (16 signatures)",
    columns = starts_with("microarray_"),
    id = "marray"
  ) %>%
  
  ### RNAseq nested spanners
  tab_spanner(
    label = "CMAP1.0",
    columns = c(RNAseq_CMAP),
    id = "rna_cmap1"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "LINCS",
    columns = c(RNAseq_NCS, RNAseq_Tau, RNAseq_WCS),
    id = "rna_cmap2"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Correlation",
    columns = c(RNAseq_Cor_pearson, RNAseq_Cor_spearman),
    id = "rna_corr"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Enrichment-based",
    columns = c(RNAseq_CMAP, RNAseq_NCS, RNAseq_Tau, RNAseq_WCS),
    id = "rna_enrich"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Correlation-based",
    columns = c(RNAseq_Cor_pearson, RNAseq_Cor_spearman),
    id = "rna_correlation"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "RNAseq (9 signatures)",
    columns = starts_with("RNAseq_"),
    id = "rna"
  ) %>%

  ### Color formatting
  data_color(
    columns = starts_with("microarray_"),
    colors = scales::col_numeric(
      palette = c("mistyrose", "red"),
      domain = c(0, max(filtered_df %>% select(starts_with("microarray_")), na.rm = TRUE))
    )
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = starts_with("RNAseq_"),
    colors = scales::col_numeric(
      palette = c("lightblue", "blue"),
      domain = c(0, max(filtered_df %>% select(starts_with("RNAseq_")), na.rm = TRUE))
    )
  )

gtsave(filtered_df, "~/Documents/GitHub/drugrep_tb/figures/drugs_methodwise_indiv_aggr_intersection_table.png", expand = 5, vwidth = 2000, vheight = 1200 )
filtered_df

```

```{r mark overlapping drugs}
# Step 1: Load the intersect_RAresult_drugs.tsv file
intersect_drugs <- read.delim("~/Documents/GitHub/drugrep_tb/results/uniformly_processed/intersect_RAresult_drugs.tsv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE, sep="\t")

# Step 1: Add the color column to the raw dataframe (before converting to gt_tbl)
filtered_df_raw <- filtered_df %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(marked_in_intersect = ifelse(unique_pert %in% intersect_drugs$drug_name, "Yes", "No"))

# Step 2: Convert to gt_tbl and apply color formatting
filtered_df <- filtered_df_raw %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "unique_pert") %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "CMAP1.0",
    columns = c(microarray_CMAP),
    id = "marray_cmap1"
  ) %>%
  # CMAP2.0 (connectivity scores)
  tab_spanner(
    label = "CMAP2.0",
    columns = c(microarray_NCS, microarray_Tau, microarray_WCS),
    id = "marray_cmap2"
  ) %>%


```

```{r either microarray or rnaseq}
# Define score groupings
score_groups <- combined_with_rank_df %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    microarray_CMAP1.0 = any(c_across(c(microarray_CMAP)) != 0),
    microarray_CMAP2.0 = any(c_across(c(microarray_NCS, microarray_Tau, microarray_WCS)) != 0),
    microarray_Correlation = any(c_across(c(microarray_Cor_pearson, microarray_Cor_spearman)) != 0),
    
    rnaseq_CMAP1.0 = any(c_across(c(RNAseq_CMAP)) != 0),
    rnaseq_CMAP2.0 = any(c_across(c(RNAseq_NCS, RNAseq_Tau, RNAseq_WCS)) != 0),
    rnaseq_Correlation = any(c_across(c(RNAseq_Cor_pearson, RNAseq_Cor_spearman)) != 0),
    
    n_pass_microarray = sum(c(microarray_CMAP1.0, microarray_CMAP2.0, microarray_Correlation)),
    n_pass_rnaseq = sum(c(rnaseq_CMAP1.0, rnaseq_CMAP2.0, rnaseq_Correlation))
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(n_pass_microarray >= 2 | n_pass_rnaseq >= 2)

# Keep only the columns of interest
filtered_either_df <- score_groups %>%
  select(MeanRank, unique_pert, all_of(score_cols)) %>%
  arrange(desc(MeanRank))

filtered_either_df <- filtered_either_df %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "unique_pert") %>%
  
  ### Microarray nested spanners
  # CMAP1.0 (classic CMAP score)
  tab_spanner(
    label = "CMAP1.0",
    columns = c(microarray_CMAP),
    id = "marray_cmap1"
  ) %>%
  
  # CMAP2.0 (connectivity scores)
  tab_spanner(
    label = "CMAP2.0",
    columns = c(microarray_NCS, microarray_Tau, microarray_WCS),
    id = "marray_cmap2"
  ) %>%
  
  # Correlation scores
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Correlation",
    columns = c(microarray_Cor_pearson, microarray_Cor_spearman),
    id = "marray_corr"
  ) %>%
  
  # Method class (enrichment-based includes CMAP1.0 + CMAP2.0)
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Enrichment-based",
    columns = c(microarray_CMAP, microarray_NCS, microarray_Tau, microarray_WCS),
    id = "marray_enrich"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Correlation-based",
    columns = c(microarray_Cor_pearson, microarray_Cor_spearman),
    id = "marray_correlation"
  ) %>%
  
  # Platform
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Microarray (16 signatures)",
    columns = starts_with("microarray_"),
    id = "marray"
  ) %>%
  
  ### RNAseq nested spanners
  tab_spanner(
    label = "CMAP1.0",
    columns = c(RNAseq_CMAP),
    id = "rna_cmap1"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "CMAP2.0",
    columns = c(RNAseq_NCS, RNAseq_Tau, RNAseq_WCS),
    id = "rna_cmap2"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Correlation",
    columns = c(RNAseq_Cor_pearson, RNAseq_Cor_spearman),
    id = "rna_corr"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Enrichment-based",
    columns = c(RNAseq_CMAP, RNAseq_NCS, RNAseq_Tau, RNAseq_WCS),
    id = "rna_enrich"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Correlation-based",
    columns = c(RNAseq_Cor_pearson, RNAseq_Cor_spearman),
    id = "rna_correlation"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "RNAseq (9 signatures)",
    columns = starts_with("RNAseq_"),
    id = "rna"
  ) %>%

  ### Color formatting
  data_color(
    columns = starts_with("microarray_"),
    colors = scales::col_numeric(
      palette = c("mistyrose", "red"),
      domain = c(0, max(filtered_either_df %>% select(starts_with("microarray_")), na.rm = TRUE))
    )
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = starts_with("RNAseq_"),
    colors = scales::col_numeric(
      palette = c("lightblue", "blue"),
      domain = c(0, max(filtered_either_df %>% select(starts_with("RNAseq_")), na.rm = TRUE))
    )
  )

gtsave(filtered_either_df, here("figureS2/drugs_methodwise_either_table_binary.html"))
filtered_either_df
```

```{r gt  table}
predicted_drugs %>%
  select(drug_name, moa, disease_area, indication) %>%
  arrange(origin) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "moa") %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Predicted drug candidates across technologies by our workflow",
    subtitle = "Grouped by Mechanism of Action (MoA)"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    drug_name = "Drug Name",
    moa = "MoA",
    disease_area = "Disease Area",
    indication = "Indication",
  ) %>%
  fmt_missing(
    columns = everything(),
    missing_text = "-"
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = "small",
    heading.title.font.size = 16,
    heading.subtitle.font.size = 12
  ) 

my_gt_table <- predicted_drugs %>%
  select(drug_name, moa, disease_area, indication) %>%
  arrange(moa) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "moa") # (same as above...)
gtsave(my_gt_table, "/Users/kewalinsamart/Documents/GitHub/drugrep_tb/figures/predicted_drugs_table.html")
```

```{r count occurrences of indications}
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)

# load original data
union_drugs_df <- read_tsv("~/Documents/GitHub/drugrep_tb/results/uniformly_processed/annotated_RAresult_TB_union.tsv")
intersecting_drugs <- readLines("~/Documents/GitHub/drugrep_tb/results/uniformly_processed/intersect_RAresult_drugs.tsv")
final_drugs_df <- union_drugs_df[union_drugs_df$drug_name %in% intersecting_drugs,]

# remove rows with missing indication or drug_name
final_drugs_df <- final_drugs_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(drug_name), !is.na(indication))

# split multi-label indications and expand rows
final_drugs_df_expanded <- final_drugs_df %>%
  separate_rows(indication, sep = "\\|")

# count occurrences of each drug × indication pair
drug_indication_counts <- final_drugs_df_expanded %>%
  count(drug_name, indication, name = "count")

# pivot to wide matrix format
drug_indication_matrix <- drug_indication_counts %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = indication, values_from = count, values_fill = 0)

# Save to TSV
write_tsv(drug_indication_matrix, "~/Documents/GitHub/drugrep_tb/results/uniformly_processed/drug_indication_matrix.tsv")

drug_indication_matrix = as.matrix(column_to_rownames(drug_indication_matrix, "drug_name"))

Heatmap(drug_indication_matrix,
        name = "Count",
        column_title = "Indication",
        row_title = "Drug",
        cluster_rows = TRUE,
        cluster_columns = TRUE)

hist(drug_indication_counts$count, breaks=5)

```

```{r count occurrences of disease area}
# split multi-label disease areas and expand rows
final_drugs_df_expanded <- final_drugs_df %>%
  separate_rows(disease_area, sep = "\\|")

# count occurrences of each drug × disease area pair
drug_disarea_counts <- final_drugs_df_expanded %>%
  count(drug_name, disease_area, name = "count")

# pivot to wide matrix format
drug_disarea_matrix <- drug_disarea_counts %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = disease_area, values_from = count, values_fill = 0)

# Save to TSV
write_tsv(drug_disarea_matrix, "~/Documents/GitHub/drugrep_tb/results/uniformly_processed/drug_disarea_matrix.tsv")

drug_disarea_matrix = as.matrix(column_to_rownames(drug_disarea_matrix, "drug_name"))

Heatmap(drug_disarea_matrix,
        name = "Count",
        column_title = "Disease Area",
        row_title = "Drug",
        cluster_rows = TRUE,
        cluster_columns = TRUE)

hist(drug_disarea_counts$count, breaks=9)
```
